entities.name: SOYBEAN
entities.description: An important oilseed crop (Glycine spp.) analyzed in the study

entities.name: GENE PRESENCE/ABSENCE VARIATION
entities.description: Variation in gene content between individuals of the same species

entities.name: SELECTION
entities.description: The process that alters the frequency of specific genes in a population

entities.name: DOMESTICATION
entities.description: The process of adapting wild plants for human use, leading to genomic modifications

entities.name: BREEDING
entities.description: The intentional mating of plants to achieve desired traits, leading to genomic modifications

entities.name: GENOMIC LANDSCAPE
entities.description: The overall structure and content of the genome

entities.name: GENETIC DIVERSITY
entities.description: The total number of genetic characteristics in the genetic makeup of a species

entities.name: PANGENOME
entities.description: An assembly representing the entire set of genes within a species, used here for 1,000 soybean accessions

entities.name: USDA SOYBEAN GERMPLASM COLLECTION
entities.description: The source of the soybean accessions used in the study

entities.name: GENE AND ALLELE FREQUENCY
entities.description: The proportion of a specific gene or allele in a population

entities.name: LEE REFERENCE GENOME ASSEMBLY
entities.description: The standard soybean genome assembly used for comparison

entities.name: PROTEIN-CODING GENES
entities.description: Genes that encode proteins, which showed a reduction in number during domestication and breeding

entities.name: AGRONOMIC TRAITS
entities.description: Desirable characteristics of crops selected for during breeding
